description_long: This object is a standard urban traffic light assembly featuring four individual signal units, each with three circular lenses arranged vertically. The housing is constructed from a dark, likely metal or hard plastic material, with each signal unit containing red, yellow, and green lenses. The lenses are slightly recessed within cylindrical hoods to minimize glare and improve visibility in bright conditions. The assembly is robust, with visible mounting brackets and bolts along the sides, suggesting it is designed for secure attachment to a pole or similar structure. The colored lenses are made of translucent material, allowing internal illumination for signaling. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for durability and clear signaling in outdoor urban environments.
description: A robust, four-unit vertical traffic light assembly with red, yellow, and green lenses in each unit, housed in a dark, durable casing. The lenses are recessed and protected by cylindrical hoods for visibility and weather resistance.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four signal units with visible red, yellow, and green lenses, each recessed within protective hoods.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the cylindrical hoods and the thickness of the housing, with no lenses visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid back panels and mounting brackets, with no signal lenses visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the cylindrical hoods extending outward, with no lenses visible.
